The **2017 [European Figure Skating Championships](/wiki/European_Figure_Skating_Championships "European Figure Skating Championships")** were held 25–29 January 2017 in [Ostrava](/wiki/Ostrava "Ostrava"), [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic").[[1]](#cite_note-ISU140615-1) Medals were awarded in the disciplines of [men's singles, ladies' singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating"), [pairs](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating"), and [ice dancing](/wiki/Ice_dancing "Ice dancing").

## Records

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2017_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Records")]

See also: [List of highest historical scores in figure skating](/wiki/List_of_highest_historical_scores_in_figure_skating "List of highest historical scores in figure skating")

The following new [ISU best scores](/wiki/ISU_Judging_System#Best_scores "ISU Judging System") were set during this competition:

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Ladies | Free skate | [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia") [Evgenia Medvedeva](/wiki/Evgenia_Medvedeva "Evgenia Medvedeva") | **150.79** | 27 January 2017 | [[2]](#cite_note-isu-progression-L-FS-2) |
| Total score | **229.71** | [[3]](#cite_note-isu-progression-L-To-3) |

## Eligibility

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2017_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Eligibility")]

Skaters were eligible for the event if they represented a European [member nation](/wiki/International_figure_skating "International figure skating") of the [International Skating Union](/wiki/International_Skating_Union "International Skating Union") and had reached the age of 15 before July 1, 2016, in their place of birth. The corresponding competition for non-European skaters is the [2017 Four Continents Championships](/wiki/2017_Four_Continents_Figure_Skating_Championships "2017 Four Continents Figure Skating Championships"). National associations selected their entries according to their own criteria but the ISU mandated that their selections achieve a minimum technical elements score (TES) at an international event prior to the European Championships.

### Minimum TES

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2017_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Minimum TES")]

| Men | 25 | 45 |
| Ladies | 20 | 36 |
| Pairs | 20 | 36 |
| Ice dance | 19 | 29 |
| Must be achieved at an ISU-recognized international event   in the ongoing or preceding season.   SP and FS scores may be attained at different events. | | |

### Number of entries per discipline

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2017_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Number of entries per discipline")]

Based on the results of the [2016 European Championships](/wiki/2016_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2016 European Figure Skating Championships"), the ISU allows each country one to three entries per discipline.

### Pairs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2017_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Pairs")]

Bronze medalists the previous two years, Tarasova/Morozov won their first European title after placing first in the short program and second in the free skate.[[23]](#cite_note-ISIpairs-23)[[24]](#cite_note-INpairs1-24)[[25]](#cite_note-INpairs2-25) Ranked third in the short and first in the free, Savchenko/Massot received silver for the second consecutive year. Bronze medalists James/Ciprès were the first French pair to win a European medal in fourteen years (since [2003](/wiki/2003_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2003 European Figure Skating Championships"), when [Sarah Abitbol](/wiki/Sarah_Abitbol "Sarah Abitbol") / [Stéphane Bernadis](/wiki/St%C3%A9phane_Bernadis "Stéphane Bernadis") took silver).
